title: High-Protein Overnight Oats
tagline: Chocolate-vanilla protein overnight oats loaded with blueberries, banana and mango — tastes like a milkshake, eats like breakfast.
macroNotes: Estimated using whey + low-fat milk. Total time is mostly the overnight chill (~6 hrs).

The fruit stack drives the carbs up fast. For a leaner cut version: 40 g oats/serving, 150 ml milk, drop the mango to 40 g/serving and use half a banana — lands around 450-500 kcal with ~40 g protein.
If your vanilla whey is heavily sweetened, banana + mango can make it very sweet — cut the banana back first.
Already high protein from whey. Want it thicker and even higher protein? Blend in 100 g hung curd/Greek yogurt per serving (+8-15 g protein, cheesecake-like texture).

items: 20 g chia seeds (optional, improves texture + satiety)
Tiny pinch salt (makes the chocolate pop)

steps.title: Mix the dry base
steps.body: In two jars (or one big one), combine 60 g vanilla whey + 16 g cocoa + 20 g chia + a pinch of cinnamon + a tiny pinch of salt. Mixing the powders first stops the protein clumping later.

steps.title: Whisk in the milk
steps.body: Add 400 ml milk slowly while whisking, so the protein dissolves smooth instead of going lumpy.

steps.title: Stir in the oats
steps.body: Stir in 100 g rolled oats until everything is evenly wet.

steps.title: Add the fruit
steps.body: Fold in the 100 g blueberries and 120 g banana. Keep the 160 g chopped mango (and extra blueberries if you like) on top so the texture stays nice — mango going mushy overnight isn't ideal.

steps.title: Chill
steps.body: Cover and refrigerate a minimum of 6 hours, ideally overnight.

steps.title: Adjust and eat
steps.body: In the morning: if too thick, loosen with a splash of milk; if too thin, stir in 5-10 g more oats. Add the peanut butter now if using.

notes: Vanilla whey + chocolate oats/cocoa tastes like a chocolate-vanilla milkshake, and vanilla is actually easier to balance than chocolate protein (which can taste artificial).
Flavour ranking: chocolate + vanilla + banana + blueberry is elite; mango is fine but the banana does most of the heavy lifting.

If using a composite product like MuscleBlaze Protein Oats instead of plain oats, cut the added whey to ~20-25 g and skip the cocoa if the oats are already chocolate-flavoured, or it becomes fake-chocolate overload.
No curd/yogurt needed — milk + whey sets perfectly on its own. Add yogurt only if you specifically want thick, pudding-like oats.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
